humanSummary.text: Architecture, frontend/API, test/CI, and focused server-route re-audits found no remaining P0/P1 blockers after the server deep-link and artifact-store repairs. P2/P3 findings were recorded as follow-up DAG nodes UTSUSHI-135 through UTSUSHI-141 and ITOTORI-121.

findings.title: Missing runtimeRunId maps to internal error
findings.description: A stale or unknown route-specific runtimeRunId can surface as an internal error instead of a typed not-found outcome.

evidence.detail: Architecture and frontend/API audits both identified the missing-run path after comparing spec/utsushi-005 against origin/main.

findings.impact: Deep-linked runtime evidence pages can report a server failure for a normal stale-link case.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Unknown runtimeRunId returns typed not-found behavior.
Dashboard renders a stale-link or not-found state without claiming evidence exists.

acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/runtime/v0.2/status with an unknown runtimeRunId returns a typed not-found response or empty not-found status instead of internal_error.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node inserted in the same branch as audit finding ingestion.

findings.title: Runtime artifact hash provenance is blurred
findings.description: Repository-generated fallback hashes are not distinguished from adapter/content-backed hashes in dashboard evidence.

evidence.detail: Architecture and test audits identified that dashboard link quality treats any non-null hash similarly.

findings.impact: Future proof manifests could overstate screenshot or artifact evidence strength.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Dashboard differentiates content-backed hashes, generated fallback hashes, and missing hashes.

acceptanceCriteria: Runtime artifact refs expose whether a hash came from adapter/content evidence or repository fallback metadata.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node inserted in the same branch as audit finding ingestion.

findings.title: Runtime status API method guard is incomplete
findings.description: Non-GET requests to /api/runtime/v0.2/status fall through as unknown route instead of method_not_allowed.

evidence.detail: All three initial audits independently called out the missing method-not-allowed coverage.

findings.impact: API diagnostics are inconsistent for the new runtime status route.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: GET behavior and unknown route behavior remain unchanged.

acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/runtime/v0.2/status returns method_not_allowed with allowed GET semantics.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node inserted in the same branch as audit finding ingestion.

findings.title: Dashboard tests lack DB-to-API smoke
findings.description: Dashboard tests use MSW fixtures; repository tests exercise DB projections, but there is no cross-layer DB-to-API-to-dashboard smoke.

evidence.detail: Test audit confirmed runtime-web-review tests are deterministic but not DB-backed.

findings.impact: MSW fixture drift could mask repository projection bugs.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Seed runtime evidence in Postgres and render the dashboard using the API response shape.
Validate API schema between DB read and dashboard render.

acceptanceCriteria: The smoke starts from repository-backed runtime evidence rows rather than hand-built dashboard JSON.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node inserted in the same branch as audit finding ingestion.

findings.title: Utsushi local gate misses touched Itotori surfaces
findings.description: just ci-utsushi can pass while app API or DB repository changes made by an Utsushi spec are not covered.

evidence.detail: Test audit identified the local gate gap; full CI and explicit ci-itotori covered this branch.

findings.impact: Future Utsushi specs may under-run local validation when they touch Itotori surfaces.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Affected validation includes app and DB checks when Utsushi specs touch those paths.

acceptanceCriteria: Changes under apps/itotori or packages/itotori-db in Utsushi-labeled specs trigger app and DB checks in the affected report.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node inserted in the same branch as audit finding ingestion.

findings.title: DB-backed local test skips need stronger visibility
findings.description: Local DB repository tests can exit successfully with DATABASE_URL absent, which is convenient but easy for agents to misread as DB validation.

evidence.detail: Test audit noted CI is covered, but local validation can report success while skipping DB-backed repository tests.

findings.impact: Local validation reports can be over-trusted when DB coverage was skipped.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: A stricter local command fails when DB-backed tests are required and DATABASE_URL is absent.

acceptanceCriteria: A stricter local validation command fails when DB-backed repository tests are required and DATABASE_URL is missing.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node inserted in the same branch as audit finding ingestion.

findings.title: Artifact symlink escape coverage is missing
findings.description: Focused re-audit found implementation uses realpath confinement, but tests do not cover symlink escape attempts under managedArtifactRoot.

evidence.detail: Focused server-route re-audit found no blocker but requested explicit symlink escape regression coverage.

findings.impact: A future refactor could remove realpath confinement without a failing test.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Symlink under managedArtifactRoot pointing outside the root is not served.
Normal managed artifact files continue to serve.

acceptanceCriteria: A symlink under managedArtifactRoot that targets a file outside the root is not served.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node inserted in the same branch as audit finding ingestion.

findings.title: Artifact dot-segment HTTP path coverage is missing
findings.description: Focused re-audit requested explicit raw HTTP path tests for literal and percent-encoded dot segments because URL parsing can normalize them before app validation.

evidence.detail: Focused server-route re-audit found no blocker but requested dot-segment status-code regression coverage.

findings.impact: Server route safety behavior around normalized dot-segment paths is undocumented.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Literal and percent-encoded dot-segment artifact-store requests cannot serve files outside configured roots.
Tests document the intended status code.

acceptanceCriteria: Percent-encoded dot-segment artifact-store requests cannot serve files outside configured roots.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node inserted in the same branch as audit finding ingestion.
